attempt: Carry Ticket 36's lesson across the problem: a proof route that only controls density, mass, or bounded statistics must be upgraded to a pointwise theorem, because a sparse exceptional object can still falsify the conjecture.

bounded_result.stress_result: A hypothetical sparse off-critical zero family can evade finite-window or measure-small tests.
bounded_result.discarded_route: tail-density or finite-zero-window evidence treated as RH proof
bounded_result.retained_route: operator/kernel positivity strong enough to exclude each off-critical zero pointwise

obstruction: Sparse or null exceptional sets are logically invisible to many aggregate metrics. A complete proof must eliminate every exceptional object, not merely show that exceptions are rare.
candidate_theorem: Every nontrivial zero is forced onto Re(s)=1/2 by a pointwise positivity or de Branges-type invariant, not by aggregate statistics.
next_experiment: Build adversarial off-critical-zero camouflage tests for each proposed kernel positivity certificate.
claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.
